title: Building images with labels can be slow
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

We're using org.opencontainers labels in our projects and I recently switched to podman. When building a container I noticed that the LABEL commands were all taking a very long time

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create the Dockerfile
    ```
    $ echo 'FROM registry.access.redhat.com/rhoar-nodejs/nodejs-10
    LABEL org.opencontainers.image.authors="<somefolks@example.org>"
    LABEL org.opencontainers.image.created=2019-06-11T19:03:37Z
    LABEL org.opencontainers.image.description="This is a test image"
    LABEL org.opencontainers.image.title=test
    LABEL org.opencontainers.image.vendor="Example.org"
    LABEL org.opencontainers.image.version=1' > Dockerfile
    ```

1. Build the container

    ```
    $ podman image ls
    REPOSITORY                                          TAG      IMAGE ID       CREATED      SIZE
    registry.access.redhat.com/rhoar-nodejs/nodejs-10   latest   f851eca3396e   8 days ago   567 MB

    $ time podman build .
    STEP 1: FROM registry.access.redhat.com/rhoar-nodejs/nodejs-10
    STEP 2: LABEL org.opencontainers.image.authors="<somefolks@example.org>"
    --> 2245759b0293c76712937bffcffc0bbe23af27337a1a6fa4414dc713bd08b08c
    STEP 3: LABEL org.opencontainers.image.created=2019-06-11T19:03:37Z
    --> b0a87a1d1c8484ab162fb6a15c447e559e52bea99675f9b67968b3a7cb88684f
    STEP 4: LABEL org.opencontainers.image.description="This is a test image"
    --> a772ba55aeef279004b6740a2dfa03219908b2a99bf9a3d06db228ce4462647b
    STEP 5: LABEL org.opencontainers.image.title=test
    --> b1e6e2d14f23d0f5e51f794869a9b84221b7f89515d6b99a5e53173fad5d3d10
    STEP 6: LABEL org.opencontainers.image.vendor="Example.org"
    --> 49cca763fb834a33cf7e67276b8799ff3f5c40313cda5bdfa32e393c7cdd1679
    STEP 7: LABEL org.opencontainers.image.version=1
    STEP 8: COMMIT
    --> d0f8d6f7ad3d48a5f67a591f8e6582cab285c50ecb650a7e36ba8795f6a655d6

    real    1m3.670s
    user    0m2.808s
    sys     0m5.200s

    $ time podman build -q .
    --> Using cache 2245759b0293c76712937bffcffc0bbe23af27337a1a6fa4414dc713bd08b08c
    --> Using cache b0a87a1d1c8484ab162fb6a15c447e559e52bea99675f9b67968b3a7cb88684f
    --> Using cache a772ba55aeef279004b6740a2dfa03219908b2a99bf9a3d06db228ce4462647b
    --> Using cache b1e6e2d14f23d0f5e51f794869a9b84221b7f89515d6b99a5e53173fad5d3d10
    --> Using cache 49cca763fb834a33cf7e67276b8799ff3f5c40313cda5bdfa32e393c7cdd1679
    --> Using cache d0f8d6f7ad3d48a5f67a591f8e6582cab285c50ecb650a7e36ba8795f6a655d6

    real    0m51.516s
    user    0m2.607s
    sys     0m5.229s
    ```

**Describe the results you received:**

The container took nearly a minute to build the first time even with the parent image already in the cache. The second time should have been much faster and it was still significantly slow.

**Describe the results you expected:**

I would have expected results more similar to Docker:

```
$ time docker build -q .
sha256:1fff910acb3de67a17b3f22cc7c1825bdb1a42b0467418e4cf35c2a4658b2293

real    0m5.359s
user    0m0.023s
sys     0m0.014s

$ time docker build -q .
sha256:1fff910acb3de67a17b3f22cc7c1825bdb1a42b0467418e4cf35c2a4658b2293

real    0m0.129s
user    0m0.019s
sys     0m0.023s
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

If I build using `FROM scratch`, the labels are processed much more quickly (although still slower than Docker):

```
$ echo 'FROM scratch
> LABEL org.opencontainers.image.authors="<somefolks@example.org>"
> LABEL org.opencontainers.image.created=2019-06-11T19:03:37Z
> LABEL org.opencontainers.image.description="This is a test image"
> LABEL org.opencontainers.image.title=test
> LABEL org.opencontainers.image.vendor="Example.org"
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.version=1> LABEL org.opencontainers.image.version=1' > Dockerfile

$ time podman build -q .
--> d47e31867bcdacb83591afc1d6e45d8480065b5c37b4d7e76eb4b175916005f0
--> 42023643b6785e2afbabe93aef99a90dd2e9f8a80457f1d937eaf3859485363c
--> a0cede419cc06a03dbf850493cbeba7a5865e82bff5199d4681fed22e9c506c9
--> 1044fd684d7f1ef7ea36d26e3f4a2fe2e3639d57ecd3cbed744abd43c5b8c0ae
--> 00802f6ddba04eb7269ec2ab2e00cd79f38ca57ba72b690b6866245439e0ce2b
--> f7cb0281eadad415b47494d1a9c8f24adfc59aa5c82c043268423efd5cbd5faf

real    0m8.938s
user    0m0.201s
sys     0m0.135s
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.4.0
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.10.4
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.10.4
  podman version: 1.4.0
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.8.3
  Conmon:
    package: 'conmon: /usr/libexec/crio/conmon'
    path: /usr/libexec/crio/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 0.2.0, commit: '
  Distribution:
    distribution: ubuntu
    version: "18.04"
  MemFree: 3420073984
  MemTotal: 16759091200
  OCIRuntime:
    package: 'cri-o-runc: /usr/bin/runc'
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev'
  SwapFree: 8171274240
  SwapTotal: 8189374464
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 8
  hostname: host
  kernel: 4.18.0-21-generic
  os: linux
  rootless: true
  uptime: 94h 6m 51.98s (Approximately 3.92 days)
registries:
  blocked: null
  insecure: null
  search:
  - docker.io
store:
  ConfigFile: /home/user/.config/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 4
  GraphDriverName: vfs
  GraphOptions: null
  GraphRoot: /home/user/.local/share/containers/storage
  GraphStatus: {}
  ImageStore:
    number: 46
  RunRoot: /tmp/1000
  VolumePath: /home/user/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Physical machine running Ubuntu 18.04.

Thanks!

comments.body:
LABEL should not be creating a new Layer, I wonder if there is a bug in buildah code that creates layers with LABEL command?
@bmaupin Could you try the same tests with the latest `buildah bud`  to see if this is just podman having an older buildah vendoring?

> @bmaupin Could you try the same tests with the latest buildah bud to see if this is just podman having an older buildah vendoring?

@rhatdan Is this what you were asking for?

```
$ buildah version
Version:         1.9.0-dev
Go Version:      go1.10.4
Image Spec:      1.0.0
Runtime Spec:    1.0.0
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:
Git Commit:
Built:           Thu May 30 19:39:16 2019
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64

$ buildah bud .
STEP 1: FROM scratch
STEP 2: LABEL org.opencontainers.image.authors="<somefolks@example.org>"
STEP 3: LABEL org.opencontainers.image.created=2019-06-11T19:03:37Z
STEP 4: LABEL org.opencontainers.image.description="This is a test image"
STEP 5: LABEL org.opencontainers.image.title=test
STEP 6: LABEL org.opencontainers.image.vendor="Example.org"
STEP 7: LABEL org.opencontainers.image.version=1
STEP 8: COMMIT
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 5f70bf18a086 done
Copying config 47ba87a7c7 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
--> 47ba87a7c79e9a7485718ef6fa68c14ac71867c6de8cfdf5b686323ef1852a91

$ podman image ls
REPOSITORY   TAG      IMAGE ID       CREATED         SIZE
<none>       <none>   47ba87a7c79e   3 seconds ago   3.26 kB

$ podman rmi 47ba87a7c79e
47ba87a7c79e9a7485718ef6fa68c14ac71867c6de8cfdf5b686323ef1852a91
```

> Can you throw a time in front of the buildah bud command to see what it looks like? 

That's much faster:

```
$ time buildah bud .
STEP 1: FROM registry.access.redhat.com/rhoar-nodejs/nodejs-10
STEP 2: LABEL org.opencontainers.image.authors="<somefolks@example.org>"
STEP 3: LABEL org.opencontainers.image.created=2019-06-11T19:03:37Z
STEP 4: LABEL org.opencontainers.image.description="This is a test image"
STEP 5: LABEL org.opencontainers.image.title=test
STEP 6: LABEL org.opencontainers.image.vendor="Example.org"
STEP 7: LABEL org.opencontainers.image.version=1
STEP 8: COMMIT
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 8783de338a11 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 16d1101dbd11 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 16dbd38945ab skipped: already exists
Copying blob 0620f0a61489 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 4c2749dbd577 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 5f70bf18a086 skipped: already exists
Copying config baa62064e9 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
--> baa62064e980062c52181e76493d7dd5653b007bf454390669f9920b69a6a028

real    0m3.720s
user    0m0.979s
sys     0m1.065s
```

That was after I had already run it and the layers were cached. But even without the cache it's much faster than podman.

> Then if you have an extra cycle, also try
>
> `time buildah bud --layers true .`

That was much slower:

```
$ time buildah bud --layers=true .
STEP 1: FROM registry.access.redhat.com/rhoar-nodejs/nodejs-10
STEP 2: LABEL org.opencontainers.image.authors="<somefolks@example.org>"
--> Using cache 409d388915b13b23248b439decfd3cde3d72c38d871445930c277af26f1043be
STEP 3: LABEL org.opencontainers.image.created=2019-06-11T19:03:37Z
--> Using cache c3fbc8bc85d8b0b3f925de8cff90d6ec11e065d06d8577a1bc3a1a75dbacde89
STEP 4: LABEL org.opencontainers.image.description="This is a test image"
--> Using cache 3744447570ee2975a3bb77391e581f68de4e8d2cc087010b0edf12c3ec2c1a43
STEP 5: LABEL org.opencontainers.image.title=test
--> Using cache 9242f99f24370f9ff1df0e6e592bd00d9e8d0c58192554f034c70f2d97025d57
STEP 6: LABEL org.opencontainers.image.vendor="Example.org"
--> Using cache 2b75f94ccfd1e05d77c49d38a16659e0783a5a9a41bd04ac8a06cf346572a3b0
STEP 7: LABEL org.opencontainers.image.version=1
--> Using cache 5060140e41781643e1e4262ae4dadc030f2c30d2e06543cd297514c9d4c0705a

real    0m59.003s
user    0m2.576s
sys     0m5.354s
```
Thanks a bunch @bmaupin , that will help us narrow down the search quite a bit.
